29	// Derive font values from registry fonts (e.g., "font-inter" -> "inter").
30	const fontValues = bodyFonts.map((f) => f.name.replace("font-", "")) as [
31	  string,
32	  ...string[],
33	]
34	const fontHeadingValues = ["inherit", ...fontValues] as const

36	export type FontValue = (typeof fontValues)[number]
37	export type FontHeadingValue = (typeof fontHeadingValues)[number]
38	
39	export function getBodyFont(font: FontValue) {
40	  return bodyFonts.find((item) => item.name === `font-${font}`)
41	}
42	
43	export function getHeadingFont(
44	  fontHeading: Exclude<FontHeadingValue, "inherit">
45	) {
46	  return headingFonts.find(
47	    (item) => item.name === `font-heading-${fontHeading}`
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	export function getInheritedHeadingFontValue(font: FontValue) {
52	  return `var(${getBodyFont(font)?.font.variable ?? "--font-sans"})`
53	}

407	export function getThemesForBaseColor(baseColorName: string) {
408	  const baseColorNames = BASE_COLORS.map((bc) => bc.name)
409	
410	  return THEMES.filter((theme) => {
411	    if (theme.name === baseColorName) {
412	      return true
413	    }
414	    return !baseColorNames.includes(theme.name)
415	  })
416	}
417	
418	export function getBase(name: BaseName) {
419	  return BASES.find((base) => base.name === name)
420	}
421	
422	export function getStyle(name: StyleName) {
423	  return STYLES.find((style) => style.name === name)
424	}
425	
426	export function getTheme(name: ThemeName) {
427	  return THEMES.find((theme) => theme.name === name)
428	}
429	
430	export function getBaseColor(name: BaseColorName) {
431	  return BASE_COLORS.find((color) => color.name === name)
432	}
433	
434	export function getIconLibrary(name: IconLibraryName) {
435	  return iconLibraries[name]
436	}
437	
438	// Builds a registry:theme item from a design system config.
439	export function buildRegistryTheme(config: DesignSystemConfig) {
440	  const baseColor = getBaseColor(config.baseColor)
441	  const theme = getTheme(config.theme)
442	
443	  if (!baseColor || !theme) {
444	    throw new Error(
445	      `Base color "${config.baseColor}" or theme "${config.theme}" not found`
446	    )
447	  }
448	
449	  // Merge base color and theme CSS vars.
450	  const lightVars: Record<string, string> = {
451	    ...(baseColor.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>),
452	    ...(theme.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>),
453	  }
454	  const darkVars: Record<string, string> = {
455	    ...(baseColor.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>),
456	    ...(theme.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>),
457	  }
458	  const themeVars: Record<string, string> = {}
459	
460	  // Apply chart color override.
461	  const chartTheme = getTheme(config.chartColor)
462	  if (chartTheme) {
463	    const chartLight = chartTheme.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>
464	    const chartDark = chartTheme.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>
465	    for (let i = 1; i <= 5; i++) {
466	      const key = `chart-${i}`
467	      if (chartLight?.[key]) lightVars[key] = chartLight[key]
468	      if (chartDark?.[key]) darkVars[key] = chartDark[key]
469	    }
470	  }
471	
472	  // Apply menu accent transformation.
473	  if (config.menuAccent === "bold") {
474	    lightVars.accent = lightVars.primary
475	    lightVars["accent-foreground"] = lightVars["primary-foreground"]
476	    darkVars.accent = darkVars.primary
477	    darkVars["accent-foreground"] = darkVars["primary-foreground"]
478	    // lightVars["sidebar-accent"] = lightVars.primary
479	    // lightVars["sidebar-accent-foreground"] = lightVars["primary-foreground"]
480	    // darkVars["sidebar-accent"] = darkVars.primary
481	    // darkVars["sidebar-accent-foreground"] = darkVars["primary-foreground"]
482	  }
483	
484	  // Apply radius transformation.
485	  if (config.radius && config.radius !== "default") {
486	    const radius = RADII.find((r) => r.name === config.radius)
487	    if (radius && radius.value) {
488	      lightVars.radius = radius.value
489	    }
490	  }
491	
492	  return {
493	    name: `${config.baseColor}-${config.theme}`,
494	    type: "registry:theme" as const,
495	    cssVars: {
496	      theme: Object.keys(themeVars).length > 0 ? themeVars : undefined,
497	      light: lightVars,
498	      dark: darkVars,
499	    },
500	  }
501	}
502	
503	// Builds a registry:base item from a design system config.
504	export function buildRegistryBase(config: DesignSystemConfig) {
505	  const baseItem = getBase(config.base)
506	  const iconLibraryItem = getIconLibrary(config.iconLibrary)
507	  const normalizedFontHeading =
508	    config.fontHeading === config.font ? "inherit" : config.fontHeading
509	
510	  if (!baseItem || !iconLibraryItem) {
511	    throw new Error(
512	      `Base "${config.base}" or icon library "${config.iconLibrary}" not found`
513	    )
514	  }
515	
516	  const registryTheme = buildRegistryTheme(config)
517	
518	  // Build dependencies.
519	  const dependencies = [
520	    `shadcn@${SHADCN_VERSION}`,
521	    "class-variance-authority",
522	    "tw-animate-css",
523	    ...(baseItem.dependencies ?? []),
524	    ...iconLibraryItem.packages,
525	  ]
526	
527	  const registryDependencies = ["utils"]
528	  const themeVars = {
529	    ...(registryTheme.cssVars?.theme ?? {}),
530	    ...(normalizedFontHeading === "inherit"
531	      ? { "--font-heading": getInheritedHeadingFontValue(config.font) }
532	      : {}),
533	  }
534	
535	  if (config.font) {
536	    registryDependencies.push(`font-${config.font}`)
537	  }
538	
539	  if (normalizedFontHeading !== "inherit") {
540	    registryDependencies.push(`font-heading-${normalizedFontHeading}`)
541	  }
542	
543	  return {
544	    name: `${config.base}-${config.style}`,
545	    extends: "none",
546	    type: "registry:base" as const,
547	    config: {
548	      style: `${config.base}-${config.style}`,
549	      iconLibrary: iconLibraryItem.name,
550	      rtl: config.rtl,
551	      menuColor: config.menuColor,
552	      menuAccent: config.menuAccent,
553	      tailwind: {
554	        baseColor: config.baseColor,
555	      },
556	    },
557	    dependencies,
558	    registryDependencies,
559	    cssVars: {
560	      ...registryTheme.cssVars,
561	      theme: Object.keys(themeVars).length > 0 ? themeVars : undefined,
562	    },
563	    css: {
564	      '@import "tw-animate-css"': {},
565	      '@import "shadcn/tailwind.css"': {},
566	      "@layer base": {
567	        "*": { "@apply border-border outline-ring/50": {} },
568	        body: { "@apply bg-background text-foreground": {} },
569	      },
570	    },
571	    ...(config.rtl && {
572	      docs: `To learn how to set up the RTL provider and fonts for your app, see https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/rtl/${config.template === "next-monorepo" ? "next" : (config.template ?? "next")}`,
573	    }),
574	  }
575	}

17	
18	## Installation
19

24	/*
25	 * build-registry.mts is the single v4 registry pipeline.
26	 *
27	 * Source of truth:
28	 * - Authored raw component/registry source lives in registry/bases/base and
29	 *   registry/bases/radix.
30	 * - Authored demo source lives in examples/base and examples/radix.
31	 * - Style tokens live in registry/styles/style-*.css.
32	 *
33	 * Persistent outputs:
34	 * - registry/bases/__index__.tsx
35	 * - registry/__index__.tsx
36	 * - examples/__index__.tsx
37	 * - styles/<base-style>/ui/*
38	 * - styles/<base-style>/ui-rtl/* for base-nova and radix-nova only
39	 * - public/r/*
40	 *
41	 * Temporary outputs:
42	 * - registry/<base-style>/*
43	 * - registry-<style>.json
44	 *
45	 * Execution order:
46	 * 1. Build registry/bases/__index__.tsx from the authored base registries.
47	 * 2. Build temporary styled registries under registry/<base-style>.
48	 * 3. Build registry/__index__.tsx for runtime lookup across legacy styles and
49	 *    generated base-style combinations.
50	 * 4. Build examples/__index__.tsx from authored demos.
51	 * 5. Export public/r/* for every style through the shadcn CLI.
52	 * 6. Copy compiled ui/* from the temporary registries into styles/<style>/ui.
53	 * 7. Build styles/<style>/ui-rtl for base-nova and radix-nova only.
54	 * 8. Format the generated persistent outputs.
55	 * 9. Clean up the temporary registry/<base-style> trees and registry-*.json.
56	 */
57	
58	const STYLE_COMBINATIONS = Array.from(BASES).flatMap((base) =>
59	  STYLES.map((style) => ({
60	    base,
61	    style,
62	    name: `${base.name}-${style.name}`,
63	    title: `${base.title} ${style.title}`,
64	  }))
65	)
66	
67	const CPU_COUNT = availableParallelism()
68	const STYLE_BUILD_CONCURRENCY = Math.max(1, Math.min(CPU_COUNT, 4))
69	const FILE_BUILD_CONCURRENCY = Math.max(4, Math.min(CPU_COUNT, 8))
70	const COPY_CONCURRENCY = Math.max(4, Math.min(CPU_COUNT, 8))
71	const CLI_BUILD_CONCURRENCY = Math.max(
72	  1,
73	  Math.min(Math.floor(CPU_COUNT / 2), 4)
74	)
75	const TRANSFORM_CACHE_VERSION = "2"
76	const CACHE_ROOT = path.join(
77	  process.cwd(),
78	  "node_modules/.cache/build-registry"
79	)
80	const TRANSFORM_CACHE_ROOT = path.join(CACHE_ROOT, "transforms")
81	const TRANSFORM_CACHE_MANIFEST_PATH = path.join(
82	  CACHE_ROOT,
83	  "transform-manifest.json"
84	)
85	
86	const transformCacheManifest = new Map<string, string>()
87	let transformCacheDirty = false
88	let prettierConfigPromise: Promise<prettier.Options | null> | null = null
89	
90	const iconProject = new Project({

92	})

96

3	import { Slot } from "radix-ui"
4	
5	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
6	
7	const buttonVariants = cva(
8	  "inline-flex shrink-0 items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-md text-sm font-medium whitespace-nowrap transition-all outline-none focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50 aria-invalid:border-destructive aria-invalid:ring-destructive/20 dark:aria-invalid:ring-destructive/40 [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4",
9	  {
10	    variants: {
11	      variant: {
12	        default: "bg-primary text-primary-foreground hover:bg-primary/90",
13	        destructive:
14	          "bg-destructive text-white hover:bg-destructive/90 focus-visible:ring-destructive/20 dark:bg-destructive/60 dark:focus-visible:ring-destructive/40",
15	        outline:
16	          "border bg-background shadow-xs hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground dark:border-input dark:bg-input/30 dark:hover:bg-input/50",
17	        secondary:
18	          "bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground hover:bg-secondary/80",
19	        ghost:
20	          "hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground dark:hover:bg-accent/50",
21	        link: "text-primary underline-offset-4 hover:underline",
22	      },
23	      size: {
24	        default: "h-9 px-4 py-2 has-[>svg]:px-3",
25	        xs: "h-6 gap-1 rounded-md px-2 text-xs has-[>svg]:px-1.5 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3",
26	        sm: "h-8 gap-1.5 rounded-md px-3 has-[>svg]:px-2.5",
27	        lg: "h-10 rounded-md px-6 has-[>svg]:px-4",
28	        icon: "size-9",
29	        "icon-xs": "size-6 rounded-md [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3",
30	        "icon-sm": "size-8",
31	        "icon-lg": "size-10",
32	      },
33	    },
34	    defaultVariants: {
35	      variant: "default",
36	      size: "default",
37	    },
38	  }
39	)
40	
41	function Button({
42	  className,
43	  variant = "default",
44	  size = "default",
45	  asChild = false,
46	  ...props
47	}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
48	  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
49	    asChild?: boolean
50	  }) {
51	  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "button"
52	
53	  return (
54	    <Comp
55	      data-slot="button"
56	      data-variant={variant}
57	      data-size={size}
58	      className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
59	      {...props}
60	    />
61	  )
62	}
63	
64	export { Button, buttonVariants }
65

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the shadcn/ui compound component patterns. Let me compile my findings into a clear report.

## Shadcn/UI Compound Component Patterns - Analysis Report

I've thoroughly examined the shadcn/ui codebase and identified the key patterns used by compound components. Here are the files I reviewed:

### Full Source Code Files Read:

1. **Accordion** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493856960-vpsxih/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx`
2. **Tabs** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493856960-vpsxih/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs.tsx`
3. **Navigation Menu** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493856960-vpsxih/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/navigation-menu.tsx`
4. **Sidebar** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493856960-vpsxih/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/sidebar.tsx`
5. **Dialog** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493856960-vpsxih/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/dialog.tsx`
6. **Button** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493856960-vpsxih/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button.tsx`
7. **Select** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493856960-vpsxih/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/select.tsx`
8. **Toggle Group** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493856960-vpsxih/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/toggle-group.tsx`
9. **Form** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493856960-vpsxih/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/form.tsx`
10. **Collapsible** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493856960-vpsxih/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/collapsible.tsx`
11. **Utilities** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493856960-vpsxih/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/lib/utils.ts`

---

## KEY PATTERNS TO REPLICATE

### 1. **Radix UI Primitives Integration**
- All compound components wrap Radix UI primitives (e.g., `Accordion as AccordionPrimitive`, `Tabs as TabsPrimitive`)
- Use the Radix primitives' native sub-components (`.Root`, `.Item`, `.Trigger`, `.Content`, etc.)
- Example:
```tsx
function Accordion({ ...props }: React.ComponentProps<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Root>) {
  return <AccordionPrimitive.Root data-slot="accordion" {...props} />
}
```

### 2. **Data Slot Attributes for Styling**
- Every component gets a unique `data-slot` attribute (e.g., `data-slot="accordion"`, `data-slot="accordion-item"`)
- Enables CSS-in-JS and Tailwind targeting
- Used consistently across all sub-components
- Example: `<AccordionPrimitive.Root data-slot="accordion" {...props} />`

### 3. **Class Variance Authority (CVA) for Variants**
- Use `cva` function from `class-variance-authority` for variant definitions
- Define variant names, sizes, and defaults
- Pass variants through props with type safety
- Example (Tabs):
```tsx
const tabsListVariants = cva(
  "group/tabs-list inline-flex w-fit items-center...",
  {
    variants: {
      variant: {
        default: "bg-muted",
        line: "gap-1 bg-transparent",
      },
    },
    defaultVariants: {
      variant: "default",
    },
  }
)
```

### 4. **Orientation Support (Horizontal/Vertical)**
- Use `data-orientation` attribute to track state
- Use CSS selectors with `group-data-[orientation=...]` for responsive styling
- Parent component passes orientation down via context or data attributes
- Example (Tabs):
```tsx
function Tabs({
  orientation = "horizontal",
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof TabsPrimitive.Root>) {
  return (
    <TabsPrimitive.Root
      data-slot="tabs"
      data-orientation={orientation}
      orientation={orientation}
      className={cn("group/tabs flex gap-2 data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-col")}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}
```

### 5. **React Context for Sub-Component Communication**
- Create typed context for compound components
- Use `React.createContext()` with proper TypeScript types
- Provide through a Provider in parent component
- Consume in child components with custom hooks
- Example (Sidebar):
```tsx
type SidebarContextProps = {
  state: "expanded" | "collapsed"
  open: boolean
  setOpen: (open: boolean) => void
  isMobile: boolean
  toggleSidebar: () => void
}

const SidebarContext = React.createContext<SidebarContextProps | null>(null)

function useSidebar() {
  const context = React.useContext(SidebarContext)
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useSidebar must be used within a SidebarProvider.")
  }
  return context
}
```

### 6. **The `cn()` Utility (clsx + tailwind-merge)**
- Utility that combines clsx and tailwind-merge
- Purpose: merge Tailwind classes without conflicts
- Use pattern: `cn("base-classes", conditionalClasses, className)`
- Implementation:
```tsx
import { clsx, type ClassValue } from "clsx"
import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge"

export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {
  return twMerge(clsx(inputs))
}
```

### 7. **Slot Pattern (asChild Pattern)**
- Use `Slot` from `radix-ui` for the "as child" polymorphism
- Allows rendering as different HTML elements
- Example (Button):
```tsx
function Button({
  className,
  variant = "default",
  size = "default",
  asChild = false,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
    asChild?: boolean
  }) {
  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "button"
  return <Comp data-slot="button" {...props} />
}
```

### 8. **Sub-Component Export Pattern**
- Export all sub-components as named exports
- Also export variant functions (e.g., `buttonVariants`, `navigationMenuTriggerStyle`)
- Example:
```tsx
export {
  Tabs,
  TabsList,
  TabsTrigger,
  TabsContent,
  tabsListVariants
}
```

### 9. **Forking Radix Sub-components**
- Each sub-component wraps its corresponding Radix primitive
- Adds `data-slot`, applies `cn()` styling, forwards props
- Simple pass-through wrapper pattern
- Example (Accordion):
```tsx
function AccordionItem({
  className,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Item>) {
  return (
    <AccordionPrimitive.Item
      data-slot="accordion-item"
      className={cn("border-b last:border-b-0", className)}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}
```

### 10. **Group Selectors for Parent Context Styling**
- Use Tailwind's `group/` and `group-data-[...]` selectors
- Enables child styling based on parent state/props
- Example (Toggle Group):
```tsx
"group-data-[variant=line]/tabs-list:bg-transparent"
"group-data-[collapsible=icon]:hidden"
"group/toggle-group flex w-fit items-center"
```

### 11. **Complex Context Scenarios (Sidebar Example)**
- State management with controlled/uncontrolled patterns
- Cookie persistence for state
- Mobile vs desktop responsive logic
- useCallback for memoization
- useMemo for context value optimization

### 12. **Extending Components with Custom Props**
- Extend Radix props with additional custom props using intersection types
- Example (Select):
```tsx
function SelectTrigger({
  className,
  size = "default",
  children,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof SelectPrimitive.Trigger> & {
  size?: "sm" | "default"
}) { ... }
```

### 13. **SVG Icon Handling**
- Use lucide-react icons
- Apply consistent sizing with data attributes and selectors
- Example patterns:
```tsx
"[&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4"
```

### 14. **Type-Safe Variant Props**
- Import `VariantProps` from `class-variance-authority`
- Use in function signatures for type safety
- Example:
```tsx
function Button({
  variant = "default",
  size = "default",
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
    asChild?: boolean
  })
```

### 15. **Focus/Accessibility Patterns**
- Consistent focus-visible styling: `focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50`
- Aria attributes used throughout
- `sr-only` class for screen reader only text
- Data attributes for state (`data-state=open/closed`)

---

## SUMMARY FOR YOUR STEPPER COMPONENT

If building a Stepper component with these patterns, you should:

1. Create a `StepperContext` to manage active step and state
2. Wrap Radix UI primitive if available, or create `Stepper.Root` wrapper
3. Create sub-components: `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperContent`, etc.
4. Support orientation with `data-orientation` and group selectors
5. Use `cva` for step status variants (active, completed, disabled, error)
6. Export all sub-components and variant functions
7. Use `data-slot` for styling hooks
8. Use `cn()` for class merging throughout
9. Apply consistent focus and accessibility patterns
10. Support `asChild` pattern via `Slot` for flexibility

All files are in the new-york-v4 registry at `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493856960-vpsxih/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/`

## KEY PATTERNS TO REPLICATE

### 1. **Radix UI Primitives Integration**
- All compound components wrap Radix UI primitives (e.g., `Accordion as AccordionPrimitive`, `Tabs as TabsPrimitive`)
- Use the Radix primitives' native sub-components (`.Root`, `.Item`, `.Trigger`, `.Content`, etc.)
- Example:
```tsx
function Accordion({ ...props }: React.ComponentProps<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Root>) {
  return <AccordionPrimitive.Root data-slot="accordion" {...props} />
}
```

### 2. **Data Slot Attributes for Styling**
- Every component gets a unique `data-slot` attribute (e.g., `data-slot="accordion"`, `data-slot="accordion-item"`)
- Enables CSS-in-JS and Tailwind targeting
- Used consistently across all sub-components
- Example: `<AccordionPrimitive.Root data-slot="accordion" {...props} />`

### 3. **Class Variance Authority (CVA) for Variants**
- Use `cva` function from `class-variance-authority` for variant definitions
- Define variant names, sizes, and defaults
- Pass variants through props with type safety
- Example (Tabs):
```tsx
const tabsListVariants = cva(
  "group/tabs-list inline-flex w-fit items-center...",
  {
    variants: {
      variant: {
        default: "bg-muted",
        line: "gap-1 bg-transparent",
      },
    },
    defaultVariants: {
      variant: "default",
    },
  }
)
```

### 4. **Orientation Support (Horizontal/Vertical)**
- Use `data-orientation` attribute to track state
- Use CSS selectors with `group-data-[orientation=...]` for responsive styling
- Parent component passes orientation down via context or data attributes
- Example (Tabs):
```tsx
function Tabs({
  orientation = "horizontal",
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof TabsPrimitive.Root>) {
  return (
    <TabsPrimitive.Root
      data-slot="tabs"
      data-orientation={orientation}
      orientation={orientation}
      className={cn("group/tabs flex gap-2 data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-col")}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}
```

### 5. **React Context for Sub-Component Communication**
- Create typed context for compound components
- Use `React.createContext()` with proper TypeScript types
- Provide through a Provider in parent component
- Consume in child components with custom hooks
- Example (Sidebar):
```tsx
type SidebarContextProps = {
  state: "expanded" | "collapsed"
  open: boolean
  setOpen: (open: boolean) => void
  isMobile: boolean
  toggleSidebar: () => void
}

const SidebarContext = React.createContext<SidebarContextProps | null>(null)

function useSidebar() {
  const context = React.useContext(SidebarContext)
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useSidebar must be used within a SidebarProvider.")
  }
  return context
}
```

### 6. **The `cn()` Utility (clsx + tailwind-merge)**
- Utility that combines clsx and tailwind-merge
- Purpose: merge Tailwind classes without conflicts
- Use pattern: `cn("base-classes", conditionalClasses, className)`
- Implementation:
```tsx
import { clsx, type ClassValue } from "clsx"
import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge"

export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {
  return twMerge(clsx(inputs))
}
```

### 7. **Slot Pattern (asChild Pattern)**
- Use `Slot` from `radix-ui` for the "as child" polymorphism
- Allows rendering as different HTML elements
- Example (Button):
```tsx
function Button({
  className,
  variant = "default",
  size = "default",
  asChild = false,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
    asChild?: boolean
  }) {
  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "button"
  return <Comp data-slot="button" {...props} />
}
```

### 8. **Sub-Component Export Pattern**
- Export all sub-components as named exports
- Also export variant functions (e.g., `buttonVariants`, `navigationMenuTriggerStyle`)
- Example:
```tsx
export {
  Tabs,
  TabsList,
  TabsTrigger,
  TabsContent,
  tabsListVariants
}
```

### 9. **Forking Radix Sub-components**
- Each sub-component wraps its corresponding Radix primitive
- Adds `data-slot`, applies `cn()` styling, forwards props
- Simple pass-through wrapper pattern
- Example (Accordion):
```tsx
function AccordionItem({
  className,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Item>) {
  return (
    <AccordionPrimitive.Item
      data-slot="accordion-item"
      className={cn("border-b last:border-b-0", className)}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}
```

### 10. **Group Selectors for Parent Context Styling**
- Use Tailwind's `group/` and `group-data-[...]` selectors
- Enables child styling based on parent state/props
- Example (Toggle Group):
```tsx
"group-data-[variant=line]/tabs-list:bg-transparent"
"group-data-[collapsible=icon]:hidden"
"group/toggle-group flex w-fit items-center"
```

### 11. **Complex Context Scenarios (Sidebar Example)**
- State management with controlled/uncontrolled patterns
- Cookie persistence for state
- Mobile vs desktop responsive logic
- useCallback for memoization
- useMemo for context value optimization

### 12. **Extending Components with Custom Props**
- Extend Radix props with additional custom props using intersection types
- Example (Select):
```tsx
function SelectTrigger({
  className,
  size = "default",
  children,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof SelectPrimitive.Trigger> & {
  size?: "sm" | "default"
}) { ... }
```

### 13. **SVG Icon Handling**
- Use lucide-react icons
- Apply consistent sizing with data attributes and selectors
- Example patterns:
```tsx
"[&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4"
```

### 14. **Type-Safe Variant Props**
- Import `VariantProps` from `class-variance-authority`
- Use in function signatures for type safety
- Example:
```tsx
function Button({
  variant = "default",
  size = "default",
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
    asChild?: boolean
  })
```

### 15. **Focus/Accessibility Patterns**
- Consistent focus-visible styling: `focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50`
- Aria attributes used throughout
- `sr-only` class for screen reader only text
- Data attributes for state (`data-state=open/closed`)

## SUMMARY FOR YOUR STEPPER COMPONENT

If building a Stepper component with these patterns, you should:

1. Create a `StepperContext` to manage active step and state
2. Wrap Radix UI primitive if available, or create `Stepper.Root` wrapper
3. Create sub-components: `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperContent`, etc.
4. Support orientation with `data-orientation` and group selectors
5. Use `cva` for step status variants (active, completed, disabled, error)
6. Export all sub-components and variant functions
7. Use `data-slot` for styling hooks
8. Use `cn()` for class merging throughout
9. Apply consistent focus and accessibility patterns
10. Support `asChild` pattern via `Slot` for flexibility

All files are in the new-york-v4 registry at `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493856960-vpsxih/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/`

1	# Contributing
2	
3	Thanks for your interest in contributing to ui.shadcn.com. We're happy to have you here.
4	
5	Please take a moment to review this document before submitting your first pull request. We also strongly recommend that you check for open issues and pull requests to see if someone else is working on something similar.
6	
7	If you need any help, feel free to reach out to [@shadcn](https://twitter.com/shadcn).
8	
9	## About this repository
10	
11	This repository is a monorepo.
12	
13	- We use [pnpm](https://pnpm.io) and [`workspaces`](https://pnpm.io/workspaces) for development.
14	- We use [Turborepo](https://turbo.build/repo) as our build system.
15	- We use [changesets](https://github.com/changesets/changesets) for managing releases.
16	
17	## Structure
18	
19	This repository is structured as follows:
20	
21	```
22	apps
23	└── v4
24	    ├── app
25	    ├── components
26	    ├── content
27	    └── registry
28	        └── new-york-v4
29	            ├── example
30	            └── ui
31	packages
32	└── shadcn
33	```
34	
35	| Path                 | Description                              |
36	| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
37	| `apps/v4/app`        | The Next.js application for the website. |
38	| `apps/v4/components` | The React components for the website.    |
39	| `apps/v4/content`    | The content for the website.             |
40	| `apps/v4/registry`   | The registry for the components.         |
41	| `packages/shadcn`    | The `shadcn` package.                    |
42	
43	## Development
44	
45	### Fork this repo
46	
47	You can fork this repo by clicking the fork button in the top right corner of this page.
48	
49	### Clone on your local machine
50	
51	```bash
52	git clone https://github.com/your-username/ui.git
53	```
54	
55	### Navigate to project directory
56	
57	```bash
58	cd ui
59	```
60	
61	### Create a new Branch
62	
63	```bash
64	git checkout -b my-new-branch
65	```
66	
67	### Install dependencies
68	
69	```bash
70	pnpm install
71	```
72	
73	### Run a workspace
74	
75	You can use the `pnpm --filter=[WORKSPACE]` command to start the development process for a workspace.
76	
77	#### Examples
78	
79	1. To run the `ui.shadcn.com` website:
80	
81	```bash
82	pnpm --filter=v4 dev
83	```
84	
85	2. To run the `shadcn` package:
86	
87	```bash
88	pnpm --filter=shadcn dev
89	```
90	
91	## Running the CLI Locally
92	
93	To run the CLI locally, you can follow the workflow:
94	
95	1. Start by running the dev server:
96	
97	   ```bash
98	   pnpm dev
99	   ```
100	
101	2. In another terminal tab, test the CLI by running:
102	
103	   ```bash
104	   pnpm shadcn
105	   ```
106	
107	   To test the CLI in a specific app, use a command like:
108	
109	   ```bash
110	   pnpm shadcn <init | add | ...> -c ~/Desktop/my-app
111	   ```
112	
113	This workflow ensures that you are running the most recent version of the registry and testing the CLI properly in your local environment.
114	
115	## Documentation
116	
117	The documentation for this project is located in the `v4` workspace. You can run the documentation locally by running the following command:
118	
119	```bash
120	pnpm --filter=v4 dev
121	```
122	
123	Documentation is written using [MDX](https://mdxjs.com). You can find the documentation files in the `apps/v4/content/docs` directory.
124	
125	## Components
126	
127	We use a registry system for developing components. You can find the source code for the components under `apps/v4/registry`. The components are organized by styles.
128	
129	```bash
130	apps
131	└── v4
132	    └── registry
133	        └── new-york-v4
134	            ├── example
135	            └── ui
136	```
137	
138	When adding or modifying components, please ensure that:
139	
140	1. You make the changes for every style.
141	2. You update the documentation.
142	3. You run `pnpm registry:build` to update the registry.
143	
144	## Commit Convention
145	
146	Before you create a Pull Request, please check whether your commits comply with
147	the commit conventions used in this repository.
148	
149	When you create a commit we kindly ask you to follow the convention
150	`category(scope or module): message` in your commit message while using one of
151	the following categories:
152	
153	- `feat / feature`: all changes that introduce completely new code or new
154	  features
155	- `fix`: changes that fix a bug (ideally you will additionally reference an
156	  issue if present)
157	- `refactor`: any code related change that is not a fix nor a feature
158	- `docs`: changing existing or creating new documentation (i.e. README, docs for
159	  usage of a lib or cli usage)
160	- `build`: all changes regarding the build of the software, changes to
161	  dependencies or the addition of new dependencies
162	- `test`: all changes regarding tests (adding new tests or changing existing
163	  ones)
164	- `ci`: all changes regarding the configuration of continuous integration (i.e.
165	  github actions, ci system)
166	- `chore`: all changes to the repository that do not fit into any of the above
167	  categories
168	
169	  e.g. `feat(components): add new prop to the avatar component`
170	
171	If you are interested in the detailed specification you can visit
172	https://www.conventionalcommits.org/ or check out the
173	[Angular Commit Message Guidelines](https://github.com/angular/angular/blob/22b96b9/CONTRIBUTING.md#-commit-message-guidelines).
174	
175	## Requests for new components
176	
177	If you have a request for a new component, please open a discussion on GitHub. We'll be happy to help you out.
178	
179	## CLI
180	
181	The `shadcn` package is a CLI for adding components to your project. You can find the documentation for the CLI [here](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/cli).
182	
183	Any changes to the CLI should be made in the `packages/shadcn` directory. If you can, it would be great if you could add tests for your changes.
184	
185	## Testing
186	
187	Tests are written using [Vitest](https://vitest.dev). You can run all the tests from the root of the repository.
188	
189	```bash
190	pnpm test
191	```
192	
193	Please ensure that the tests are passing when submitting a pull request. If you're adding new features, please include tests.
194	

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report. ## Summary: Demo/Example Files Structure in shadcn/ui Based on my exploration of the codebase, here's the complete structure and patterns for how demo files work in shadcn/ui: --- ### 1. **Demo File Locations** Demo files live in two main directories: **Authored Source:** - `/apps/v4/examples/base/` - Base UI demos - `/apps/v4/examples/radix/` - Radix UI demos - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/` - Base registry reference - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/` - Radix registry reference - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` - New York style (legacy, generated) **Generated Output:** - `/apps/v4/examples/__index__.tsx` - Dynamically generated index --- ### 2. **Naming Convention for Demo Files** **Pattern:** `{component-name}-{variant}.tsx` Examples from `/apps/v4/examples/radix/`: - `accordion-demo.tsx` - Main/default demo - `accordion-basic.tsx` - Basic variant - `accordion-card.tsx` - Card variant - `accordion-borders.tsx` - Borders variant - `accordion-disabled.tsx` - Disabled variant - `accordion-multiple.tsx` - Multiple variant - `accordion-rtl.tsx` - RTL variant For components with only one demo (like button), it's simply: - `button-demo.tsx` --- ### 3. **Demo File Format (Exact Pattern)** **File:** `/apps/v4/examples/radix/accordion-demo.tsx` ```tsx import { Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion" export default function AccordionDemo() { return ( Product Information

Our flagship product combines cutting-edge technology with sleek design. Built with premium materials, it offers unparalleled performance and reliability.

{/* more items... */} ) } ``` **Key characteristics:** - Always `export default function {ComponentName}Demo() {}` - Single return statement with JSX - Imports from `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/{component}` - Uses semantic HTML and accessibility attributes - Includes realistic content (not placeholder "foo bar" text) - No props, state, or interactivity unless needed to demonstrate the component **Alternative format for more complex demos:** File: `/apps/v4/examples/radix/tabs-demo.tsx` ```tsx export default function TabsDemo() { return (
Account Password {/* content */}
) } ``` --- ### 4. **Registry Configuration (How Demos Are Registered)** **File:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` ```typescript import { type Registry } from "shadcn/schema" export const examples: Registry["items"] = [ { name: "accordion-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["accordion"], files: [ { path: "examples/accordion-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], }, { name: "button-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["button"], files: [ { path: "examples/button-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], }, // ... more examples ] ``` **Structure:** - `name` - kebab-case identifier (e.g., `accordion-demo`) - `type` - Always `"registry:example"` - `registryDependencies` - Array of component names this demo depends on - `files.path` - Relative path to the demo file - `files.type` - Always `"registry:example"` **File location:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/examples/_registry.ts` The registry is then imported and merged in the main registry file: **File:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/registry.ts` ```typescript import { examples } from "./examples/_registry" export const registry = { name: "shadcn/ui", items: [ ...ui, ...examples, // ... other registry items ] } ``` --- ### 5. **Documentation/MDX Integration** Demos are referenced in component documentation MDX files: **File:** `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/accordion.mdx` ```mdx --- title: Accordion description: A vertically stacked set of interactive headings that each reveal a section of content. base: radix component: true --- ## Installation ... ``` **Key attributes:** - `name` - Must match the demo registry name exactly (e.g., `accordion-demo`) - `styleName` - Style variant (e.g., `radix-nova`, `base-nova`) - `align` - Content alignment in preview - `previewClassName` - Optional tailwind classes for the preview container --- ### 6. **Build Process & Registry Generation** **Build Script:** `/apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts` The build process: 1. **Scans demo directories** (lines 613-637): ```typescript const allEntries = await fs.readdir(baseDir, { withFileTypes: true }) const files = allEntries .filter((entry) => entry.isFile() && entry.name.endsWith(".tsx")) .map((entry) => entry.name) ``` 2. **Generates `/apps/v4/examples/__index__.tsx`** (lines 639-682): - Creates dynamic imports with React.lazy - Maps each `.tsx` file to an exported component - Automatically discovers all demo files without manual registration 3. **Runtime lookup** enables demos to be loaded dynamically **Package scripts in package.json:** ```json "registry:build": "pnpm --filter=shadcn build && bun run ./scripts/build-registry.mts" ``` This is run automatically during `npm run dev` and `npm run build`. --- ### 7. **Key Differences: new-york-v4 Style** The `new-york-v4` registry is **legacy/generated** from the base registries: **File:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` (generated) ```typescript export const examples: Registry["items"] = [ { name: "accordion-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["accordion"], files: [ { path: "examples/accordion-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], }, // ... no title field in generated versions ] ``` **Difference:** Generated registries don't have the `title` field that base registries have. --- ### 8. **Complete Example Pattern for Stepper Component** To create a Stepper demo, you would: 1. **Create demo file:** `/apps/v4/examples/radix/stepper-demo.tsx` ```tsx import { Stepper, StepperItem, StepperContent } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper" export default function StepperDemo() { return ( Step 1 content Step 2 content ) } ``` 2. **Register in:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` ```typescript { name: "stepper-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper"], files: [ { path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], } ``` 3. **Reference in MDX:** `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/stepper.mdx` ```mdx ``` 4. **Build:** Run `pnpm registry:build` to generate the index and make it discoverable --- ### 9. **Key Files Summary** | Path | Purpose | |------|---------| | `/apps/v4/examples/radix/` | **Authored demo source** (main directory) | | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` | Registry configuration for radix demos | | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/registry.ts` | Main registry importing examples | | `/apps/v4/examples/__index__.tsx` | Auto-generated index (runtime lookup) | | `/apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts` | Build script that generates everything | | `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/` | MDX docs referencing demos | --- This is the exact pattern you need to replicate for your Stepper component demo!

`/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` (generated) ```typescript export const examples: Registry["items"] = [ { name: "accordion-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["accordion"], files: [ { path: "examples/accordion-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], }, // ... no title field in generated versions ] ``` **Difference:** Generated registries don't have the `title` field that base registries have. --- ### 8. **Complete Example Pattern for Stepper Component** To create a Stepper demo, you would: 1. **Create demo file:** `/apps/v4/examples/radix/stepper-demo.tsx` ```tsx import { Stepper, StepperItem, StepperContent } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper" export default function StepperDemo() { return ( Step 1 content Step 2 content ) } ``` 2. **Register in:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` ```typescript { name: "stepper-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper"], files: [ { path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], } ``` 3. **Reference in MDX:** `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/stepper.mdx` ```mdx ``` 4. **Build:** Run `pnpm registry:build` to generate the index and make it discoverable --- ### 9. **Key Files Summary** | Path | Purpose | |------|---------| | `/apps/v4/examples/radix/` | **Authored demo source** (main directory) | | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` | Registry configuration for radix demos | | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/registry.ts` | Main registry importing examples | | `/apps/v4/examples/__index__.tsx` | Auto-generated index (runtime lookup) | | `/apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts` | Build script that generates everything | | `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/` | MDX docs referencing demos | --- This is the exact pattern you need to replicate for your Stepper component demo!

## Comprehensive shadcn/ui Codebase Analysis

### 1. Overall Project Structure

The shadcn/ui project is organized as a **monorepo** using pnpm workspaces and Turborepo for build orchestration:

```
Root Directory
├── apps/
│   └── v4/                    # Main v4 website and registry
│       ├── app/               # Next.js application
│       ├── components/        # React components for the website UI
│       ├── content/           # MDX documentation files
│       ├── registry/          # Component registry (source of truth)
│       ├── examples/          # Generated examples index
│       ├── hooks/             # Custom React hooks
│       ├── lib/               # Utility functions
│       ├── public/            # Static assets
│       ├── styles/            # CSS and styling
│       ├── scripts/           # Build and registry scripts
│       ├── package.json       # v4 workspace config
│       ├── components.json    # shadcn CLI config
│       ├── tsconfig.json      # TypeScript config
│       └── registry.json      # Generated registry output
├── packages/
│   └── shadcn/                # The shadcn CLI package
├── templates/                 # Framework templates for the CLI
├── scripts/                   # Root-level utility scripts
├── package.json               # Root workspace config
├── pnpm-workspace.yaml        # Workspace definition
└── turbo.json                 # Turborepo configuration
```

### 2. Registry System Overview

The registry is a **sophisticated two-tier system**:

#### **Source of Truth (Authored Code)**
- **Base Components**: `apps/v4/registry/bases/` contains authored component source for each base (radix, base)
- **Examples/Demos**: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` contains demo components
- **Generated Outputs**: Built via `pnpm registry:build`

#### **Registry Files Structure** (`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/`)

The main registry entry point is at `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts`:

```typescript
export const registry = {
  name: "shadcn/ui",
  homepage: "https://ui.shadcn.com",
  items: z.array(registryItemSchema).parse([
    // Indexed and merged from multiple sources:
    ...ui,           // from ui/_registry.ts
    ...blocks,       // from blocks/_registry.ts
    ...charts,       // from charts/_registry.ts
    ...lib,          // from lib/_registry.ts
    ...hooks,        // from hooks/_registry.ts
    ...themes,       // from themes
    ...examples,     // from examples/_registry.ts
    ...internal,     // internal utilities
  ])
}
```

### 3. File Naming Conventions

**Components** (UI):
- File: `registry/new-york-v4/ui/component-name.tsx`
- Registry name: `"component-name"` (kebab-case)
- Example: `button.tsx` → registered as `"button"`

**Examples/Demos**:
- File: `registry/new-york-v4/examples/component-name-demo.tsx`
- Registry name: `"component-name-demo"` or `"component-name-variant"`
- Examples: `button-demo.tsx`, `button-default.tsx`, `button-as-child.tsx`

**Blocks** (Complex Components):
- Directory: `registry/new-york-v4/blocks/block-name/`
- Registry name: `"block-name"`
- Files: Main file `page.tsx`, supporting files in `components/` subdirectory
- Example: `sidebar-01/` contains `page.tsx` and `components/*.tsx`

**Hooks**:
- File: `registry/new-york-v4/hooks/use-hook-name.ts`
- Registry name: `"use-hook-name"`
- Example: `use-mobile.ts`

**Library Utilities**:
- File: `registry/new-york-v4/lib/utils.ts`
- Registry name: `"utils"`

### 6. Registry Schema and Types

The registry uses **Zod validation** with types from `shadcn/schema`. Each registry entry follows this structure:

#### **UI Component Entry** (from `ui/_registry.ts`):
```typescript

  name: "button",

    {

}
```

#### **Example Entry** (from `examples/_registry.ts`):

    },
  ],
}
```

#### **Block Entry** (from `blocks/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
{
  name: "dashboard-01",
  type: "registry:block",
  description: "A dashboard with sidebar, charts and data table.",
  dependencies: ["@dnd-kit/core", "@tabler/icons-react", "@tanstack/react-table", "zod"],
  registryDependencies: [
    "sidebar", "breadcrumb", "separator", "label", 
    "chart", "card", "select", "tabs", "table", 
    "toggle-group", "badge", "button", "checkbox", 
    "dropdown-menu", "drawer", "input", "avatar", 
    "sheet", "sonner",
  ],
  files: [
    { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/page.tsx", type: "registry:page", target: "app/dashboard/page.tsx" },
    { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/data.json", type: "registry:file", target: "app/dashboard/data.json" },
    { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/components/app-sidebar.tsx", type: "registry:component" },
    { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/components/data-table.tsx", type: "registry:component" },
    // ... more component files
  ],
  categories: ["dashboard"],
  meta: { iframeHeight: "1000px" },
}
```

#### **Hook Entry** (from `hooks/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
{
  name: "use-mobile",
  type: "registry:hook",
  files: [
    {
      path: "hooks/use-mobile.ts",
      type: "registry:hook",
    },
  ],
}
```

#### **Lib Entry** (from `lib/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
{
  name: "utils",
  type: "registry:lib",
  dependencies: ["clsx", "tailwind-merge"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "lib/utils.ts",
      type: "registry:lib",
    },
  ],
}
```

### 7. Configuration Files

**components.json** (`apps/v4/components.json`):
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json",
  "style": "new-york",
  "rsc": true,
  "tsx": true,
  "tailwind": {
    "config": "",
    "css": "app/globals.css",
    "baseColor": "neutral",
    "cssVariables": true,
    "prefix": ""
  },
  "aliases": {
    "components": "@/components",
    "utils": "@/lib/utils",
    "ui": "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui",
    "lib": "@/lib",
    "hooks": "@/hooks"
  },
  "iconLibrary": "lucide"
}
```

**registry/config.ts** - Design System Configuration:
- Defines `BASES` (radix, base)
- Defines `STYLES` (vega, nova, maia, lyra, mira, luma)
- Defines `BASE_COLORS` and `THEMES`
- Exports `DesignSystemConfig` schema and validation functions
- Contains `PRESETS` for common style combinations

**registry/bases.ts** - Base Definitions:
```typescript
export const BASES = [
  {
    name: "radix",
    type: "registry:style",
    title: "Radix UI",
    description: "Optimized for fast development, easy maintenance, and accessibility.",
    dependencies: ["radix-ui"],
  },
  {
    name: "base",
    type: "registry:style",
    title: "Base UI",
    description: "Components for building accessible web apps and design systems.",
    dependencies: ["@base-ui/react"],
  },
]
```

### 8. Build and Registry Generation

**Main Script**: `apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts`

The build process follows this order:
1. Build `registry/bases/__index__.tsx` from authored base registries
2. Build temporary styled registries under `registry/<base-style>/`
3. Build `registry/__index__.tsx` for runtime lookup
4. Build `examples/__index__.tsx` from authored demos
5. Export `public/r/*` for every style through shadcn CLI
6. Copy compiled `ui/*` from temporary registries into `styles/<style>/ui`
7. Build `styles/<style>/ui-rtl` for base-nova and radix-nova only
8. Format generated persistent outputs
9. Clean up temporary files

**Key npm scripts** (`apps/v4/package.json`):
```json
{
  "registry:build": "pnpm --filter=shadcn build && bun run ./scripts/build-registry.mts",
  "registry:capture": "tsx --tsconfig ./tsconfig.scripts.json ./scripts/capture-registry.mts"
}
```

### 9. File Types in Registry

Registry items support multiple file types with automatic target path resolution:

| File Type | Default Target | Purpose |
|-----------|-----------------|---------|
| `registry:ui` | `components/ui/{filename}` | UI component files |
| `registry:component` | `components/{filename}` | Complex component files |
| `registry:block` | `components/{filename}` | Block component files |
| `registry:example` | `components/{filename}` | Example/demo files |
| `registry:page` | Custom via `target` | Full page components (e.g., `app/dashboard/page.tsx`) |
| `registry:file` | Custom via `target` | Static files (e.g., JSON data files) |
| `registry:hook` | `hooks/{filename}` | Custom React hooks |
| `registry:lib` | `lib/{filename}` | Utility/library functions |

### 10. Import Path Resolution

The registry includes intelligent import path fixing via `lib/registry.ts`:

- Imports from `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/component` → `@/components/ui/component`
- Imports from `@/styles/*/ui/component` → `@/components/ui/component`
- Imports from `@/registry/*/hooks/hook` → `@/hooks/hook`
- Imports from `@/registry/*/lib/utility` → `@/lib/utility`

### Summary: Exact Format for Registering a New Component

To add a new component "my-component":

**1. Create the component file:**
```
apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/my-component.tsx
```

**2. Add to `ui/_registry.ts`:**
```typescript
{
  name: "my-component",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],  // Add NPM dependencies if needed
  registryDependencies: ["button"],  // Other registry items it depends on
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/my-component.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
}
```

**3. Create demo file:**
```
apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/my-component-demo.tsx
```

**4. Add to `examples/_registry.ts`:**
```typescript
{
  name: "my-component-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["my-component"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/my-component-demo.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
}
```

**5. Build the registry:**
```bash
pnpm registry:build
```

The build process will automatically:
- Validate all entries against the Zod schema
- Generate the main registry index (`__index__.tsx`)
- Transform imports to correct paths
- Generate the `registry.json` output file

2. Build temporary styled registries under `registry/<base-style>/`
3. Build `registry/__index__.tsx` for runtime lookup
4. Build `examples/__index__.tsx` from authored demos
5. Export `public/r/*` for every style through shadcn CLI
6. Copy compiled `ui/*` from temporary registries into `styles/<style>/ui`
7. Build `styles/<style>/ui-rtl` for base-nova and radix-nova only
8. Format generated persistent outputs
9. Clean up temporary files

**Key npm scripts** (`apps/v4/package.json`):
```json
{
  "registry:build": "pnpm --filter=shadcn build && bun run ./scripts/build-registry.mts",
  "registry:capture": "tsx --tsconfig ./tsconfig.scripts.json ./scripts/capture-registry.mts"
}
```

### 9. File Types in Registry

Registry items support multiple file types with automatic target path resolution:

| File Type | Default Target | Purpose |
|-----------|-----------------|---------|
| `registry:ui` | `components/ui/{filename}` | UI component files |
| `registry:component` | `components/{filename}` | Complex component files |
| `registry:block` | `components/{filename}` | Block component files |
| `registry:example` | `components/{filename}` | Example/demo files |
| `registry:page` | Custom via `target` | Full page components (e.g., `app/dashboard/page.tsx`) |
| `registry:file` | Custom via `target` | Static files (e.g., JSON data files) |
| `registry:hook` | `hooks/{filename}` | Custom React hooks |
| `registry:lib` | `lib/{filename}` | Utility/library functions |

### 10. Import Path Resolution

The registry includes intelligent import path fixing via `lib/registry.ts`:

- Imports from `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/component` → `@/components/ui/component`
- Imports from `@/styles/*/ui/component` → `@/components/ui/component`
- Imports from `@/registry/*/hooks/hook` → `@/hooks/hook`
- Imports from `@/registry/*/lib/utility` → `@/lib/utility`

### Summary: Exact Format for Registering a New Component

To add a new component "my-component":

**1. Create the component file:**
```
apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/my-component.tsx
```

**2. Add to `ui/_registry.ts`:**
```typescript
{
  name: "my-component",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],  // Add NPM dependencies if needed
  registryDependencies: ["button"],  // Other registry items it depends on
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/my-component.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
}
```

**3. Create demo file:**
```
apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/my-component-demo.tsx
```

**4. Add to `examples/_registry.ts`:**
```typescript
{
  name: "my-component-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["my-component"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/my-component-demo.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
}
```

**5. Build the registry:**
```bash
pnpm registry:build
```

The build process will automatically:
- Validate all entries against the Zod schema
- Generate the main registry index (`__index__.tsx`)
- Transform imports to correct paths
- Generate the `registry.json` output file

7	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
8	import { toggleVariants } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/toggle"
9	
10	const ToggleGroupContext = React.createContext<
11	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
12	    spacing?: number
13	  }
14	>({
15	  size: "default",
16	  variant: "default",
17	  spacing: 0,
18	})
19	
20	function ToggleGroup({
21	  className,
22	  variant,
23	  size,
24	  spacing = 0,
25	  children,
26	  ...props
27	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root> &
28	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
29	    spacing?: number
30	  }) {
31	  return (
32	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root
33	      data-slot="toggle-group"
34	      data-variant={variant}
35	      data-size={size}
36	      data-spacing={spacing}
37	      style={{ "--gap": spacing } as React.CSSProperties}
38	      className={cn(
39	        "group/toggle-group flex w-fit items-center gap-[--spacing(var(--gap))] rounded-md data-[spacing=default]:data-[variant=outline]:shadow-xs",
40	        className
41	      )}
42	      {...props}
43	    >
44	      <ToggleGroupContext.Provider value={{ variant, size, spacing }}>
45	        {children}
46	      </ToggleGroupContext.Provider>
47	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root>
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	function ToggleGroupItem({
52	  className,
53	  children,
54	  variant,
55	  size,
56	  ...props
57	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item> &
58	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants>) {
59	  const context = React.useContext(ToggleGroupContext)
60	
61	  return (
62	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item
63	      data-slot="toggle-group-item"
64	      data-variant={context.variant || variant}
65	      data-size={context.size || size}
66	      data-spacing={context.spacing}
67	      className={cn(

69	          variant: context.variant || variant,
70	          size: context.size || size,
71	        }),
72	        "w-auto min-w-0 shrink-0 px-3 focus:z-10 focus-visible:z-10",
73	        "data-[spacing=0]:rounded-none data-[spacing=0]:shadow-none data-[spacing=0]:first:rounded-l-md data-[spacing=0]:last:rounded-r-md data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:border-l-0 data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:first:border-l",
74	        className
75	      )}
76	      {...props}
77	    >
78	      {children}
79	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item>
80	  )

content.thinking:
Now I have all the information I need to create a comprehensive plan. Let me design the plan for the Stepper component.

## Summary of Key Facts:

1. **File locations:**
   - Component: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`
   - Demo: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`
   - Register component in: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`
   - Register demo in: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`

2. **Component patterns:**
   - `"use client"` at top
   - `import * as React from "react"`
   - `import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"`
   - Uses `data-slot` attributes on all sub-components
   - Functions (not arrow functions, no forwardRef)
   - `className` + `...props` spread pattern
   - Named exports at bottom

3. **Context pattern** (from toggle-group):
   - `React.createContext<ContextType>(defaultValue)`
   - Provide context in the parent component

's render
   - `React.useContext(Context)` in children

4. **Orientation pattern** (from tabs):
   - Parent gets `data-orientation` attribute
   - Children use `group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/parent:...` CSS selectors

5. **Registry entries:**
   - UI: `name`, `type: "registry:ui"`, `dependencies` (if npm deps), `registryDependencies` (registry deps), `files`
   - Example: `name`, `type: "registry:example"`, `registryDependencies`, `files`

6. **No Radix UI primitive for Stepper** — This needs to be built from scratch with React context

Now I'm mapping out the Stepper structure. Since there's no existing Radix primitive to build on, I'll need to create the root container with context management, individual step items that track their state, and clickable triggers that handle navigation between steps.

For the sub-components, I need a trigger for interaction, title and description for labeling, a separator to visually divide steps, and a content panel to display step-specific information. The state management will track which step is active and whether steps are completed, with support for both horizontal and vertical layouts through context and CSS selectors.

I'm sketching out the context structure to pass active step state and orientation down the tree, then designing the component API to follow the same pattern as the accordion and tabs components.

Now I'm weighing whether to use index-based step numbering (where steps are implicitly numbered by DOM order) versus a value-based approach like accordion. Index-based would require dynamically counting children, but value-based adds complexity since steppers naturally work with numeric step progression. Looking at how other libraries handle this, they typically pass a steps array to the root component, which gives explicit control over step definitions and ordering. I need to figure out how to track step completion states. Rather than requiring users to pass the full step list upfront, I can have the root context maintain a `completedSteps` set that gets updated whenever a step is visited. For linear steppers, clicking a trigger would only work if all previous steps are completed, otherwise it's disabled.

Actually, the simpler approach is to let users control completion explicitly—each `StepperItem` accepts a `completed` prop, and the item determines its state by comparing its value to the root's active value. This keeps the component flexible and matches shadcn/ui's philosophy.

Defining the context types...

Building the usage example...

But I'm realizing this approach has some limitations.  The `StepperPanel` being nested inside `StepperItem` follows an accordion pattern where content lives directly with each step, whereas most wizard flows use a tabs-style layout with triggers at the top and a single content area below. The spec mentions "step content panels" which could go either way, but for a proper multi-step wizard, separating the triggers from the content makes more sense. I think the cleanest approach is to make `StepperPanel` render conditionally based on whether its parent step is active, giving users flexibility in how they structure their content. I'll use a compound component pattern with a root `Stepper` component that manages state via context, individual `StepperItem` components for each step, and `StepperTrigger` and `StepperPanel` sub-components that respond to that context.

For the controlled/uncontrolled behavior, I'll follow shadcn/ui's pattern with `value`/`defaultValue`/`onValueChange` props, implementing the state management directly rather than relying on an external hook. I'll go with letting users explicitly pass a `completed` prop on each `StepperItem` since it's simpler and gives them full control over the completion state. Then the item's state is determined by checking if it matches the active value (making it "active"), if it's marked as completed (making it "completed"), or otherwise it's "inactive".

For the visual design, the trigger will display a step number or icon in a circle with styling that changes based on state—filled for active, a checkmark for completed, and outlined for inactive. I'll use `data-state` attributes to handle the styling. The separator between steps will be a line that connects the indicators, and its appearance should reflect whether the adjacent steps are completed.

Now I'm thinking through the component structure: StepperTrigger acts as a button that internally renders a circle indicator showing either the step number or a checkmark icon, plus a content area where StepperTitle and StepperDescription get placed. The trigger will use Tailwind classes to style the circle and text layout, with the indicator and content as separate spans inside the button.

The tricky part is figuring out what number to display in the circle. I could derive it from an `index` prop on StepperItem, or count items automatically in the root component, but the simplest solution is having the user explicitly pass the step number or indicator content. I'm leaning toward a simpler approach where the indicator shows a checkmark when complete, an empty circle when inactive, and a filled circle when active—no step numbers by default. But since many steppers do display step numbers, I could let users optionally pass content into the indicator region for flexibility. The cleanest API would follow shadcn/ui conventions and let StepperTrigger handle the indicator rendering while accepting children for custom content.

Building out the component structure...

I'm setting up the context system with a main Stepper context to track the current value and orientation, plus a StepperItem context for individual step state management. Then I'm creating custom hooks to access these contexts safely, throwing errors if they're used outside their proper providers. Now I need to define the actual components that use these contexts.

I'm building the Stepper component to handle both controlled and uncontrolled value states, managing the step navigation through a callback that respects whether the component is controlled or uncontrolled...

Now I'm setting up the StepperItem component to determine if a step is active by comparing its value against the current active value from context, and I'll use that to style and manage the step's state. Writing the trigger button... Writing component styles... Finishing the StepperPanel component that checks if the step is active and conditionally renders its content, then exporting all the stepper components. I'm noticing a potential issue though—the StepperSeparator relies on the useStepper hook which means it needs to be used within the Stepper context, but since it sits between StepperItems rather than inside them, that should work fine. The StepperPanel correctly uses useStepperItem since it belongs inside a StepperItem. I'm reconsidering the StepperTrigger design—it currently wraps children in a div, but that might be unnecessary since StepperTitle and StepperDescription handle their own styling. The trigger also needs to render both the indicator and the content area together.

For the panel visibility, I'm weighing the hidden attribute against CSS-based approaches. The hidden attribute is simpler and appropriate for progressive disclosure, so I'll go with that rather than trying to use Tailwind's data attribute selectors or checking state directly.

Now I'm sketching out a demo that shows a three-step wizard flow with account creation, profile setup, and review stages. I'm building an interactive example where users can navigate between steps using next and back buttons, pulling in the stepper components and button UI.

Setting up the state management...

Completing the back navigation handler and rendering the stepper component with step items that display titles and descriptions, along with panels for each step's content.

Now I'm noticing a potential issue with how the `StepperPanel` visibility works when it's nested inside `StepperItem` — I need to think through whether the hidden state is being applied correctly to show only the active step's content. the stepper is horizontal with the panel below the trigger, the layout might be off. Let me think about the layout:

For horizontal:
```
[Trigger 1] ---- [Trigger 2] ---- [Trigger 3]
[Content for active step]
```

The content for all steps could be rendered inside each `StepperItem` in "vertical" mode (below the trigger). For horizontal mode, typically the content is in a separate section below all triggers.

This creates a design challenge: if `StepperPanel` is inside `StepperItem`, and items are laid out horizontally (flex-row), each panel would be inside its column, making horizontal layout awkward.

One solution: in horizontal mode, `StepperPanel` renders absolutely or is expected to be placed outside the main stepper row. But that breaks the compound component pattern.

Alternative: accept that `StepperPanel` works better in vertical mode within the item, and for horizontal mode, users typically render content outside the `Stepper` component altogether. Looking at common stepper patterns, I'm seeing that accordion-style implementations work well in vertical layouts...

Now I have everything I need to design the plan. Let me also check if there's a `button.tsx` in the UI to understand if I should depend on it in the demo, and read it to understand the pattern for potential use in the stepper demo.

Actually, I have enough information. Let me now run the Plan agent to design the implementation.

I need a detailed implementation plan for a new "Stepper" compound component in a shadcn/ui monorepo. Do NOT write any code — only plan.

## Context

This is the shadcn/ui monorepo at `apps/v4/`. The registry system works like this:
- UI components live at: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/`
- Demo examples live at: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/`
- UI components are registered in: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`
- Examples are registered in: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`
- The constraint: **Do NOT modify any existing component files**. Only create new files and add registry entries.

## Existing Component Patterns (must follow exactly)

**Accordion pattern** (`accordion.tsx`):
```tsx
"use client"
import * as React from "react"
import { SomePrimitive } from "radix-ui"
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

function Accordion({ ...props }) { return <Primitive.Root data-slot="accordion" {...props} /> }
function AccordionItem({ className, ...props }) { return <Primitive.Item data-slot="accordion-item" className={cn("...", className)} {...props} /> }
// ... more sub-components
export { Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent }
```

**Tabs pattern** (`tabs.tsx`) - for orientation support:
```tsx
function Tabs({ className, orientation = "horizontal", ...props }) {
  return <TabsPrimitive.Root data-slot="tabs" data-orientation={orientation} orientation={orientation}
    className={cn("group/tabs flex gap-2 data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-col", className)} {...props} />
}
// child components use: group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/tabs:... group-data-[orientation=vertical]/tabs:...
```

**ToggleGroup pattern** (`toggle-group.tsx`) - for React context:
```tsx
const ToggleGroupContext = React.createContext<{ variant?: string, size?: string }>({ ... })

function ToggleGroup({ variant, size, children, ...props }) {
  return <Primitive.Root ...>
    <ToggleGroupContext.Provider value={{ variant, size }}>
      {children}
    </ToggleGroupContext.Provider>
  </Primitive.Root>
}

function ToggleGroupItem(...) {
  const context = React.useContext(ToggleGroupContext)
  // uses context values
}
```

**Key conventions**:
- `"use client"` at top
- `import * as React from "react"`
- `data-slot="component-name"` on every sub-component's root element
- `cn("...", className)` for class merging
- `cva` for variants when needed
- No Radix UI primitive exists for Stepper — must build from scratch with custom React context
- `import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"`
- Components don't use `React.forwardRef` — they use `React.ComponentProps<"div">` pattern

**Registry entry format** (`ui/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
{
  name: "stepper",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["class-variance-authority", "lucide-react"],
  files: [{ path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }],
}
```

**Example entry format** (`examples/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
{
  name: "stepper-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["stepper"],
  files: [{ path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example" }],
}
```

**Demo file format** (`examples/stepper-demo.tsx`):
```tsx
import { Stepper, StepperItem, ... } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper"
export default function StepperDemo() {
  return ( ... )
}
```

## Requirements for the Stepper Component

Build a compound component for multi-step wizards and onboarding flows with:

**Sub-components:**
1. `Stepper` — root container, manages active step state via React context, supports `orientation` (horizontal/vertical), controlled (`value`) and uncontrolled (`defaultValue`) patterns
2. `StepperItem` — individual step wrapper, receives `value` prop (like AccordionItem), provides step-level context (its index, value, completion state)
3. `StepperTrigger` — clickable step header button (like AccordionTrigger) — clicking it sets that step as active
4. `StepperTitle` — title text for the step
5. `StepperDescription` — subtitle/description text for the step
6. `StepperSeparator` — visual connector line between steps (horizontal bar or vertical bar depending on orientation)
7. `StepperContent` — the panel showing content for a step when it is active (like TabsContent/AccordionContent)

**State & Behavior:**
- Managed with React context (like ToggleGroup pattern — but more complex since Stepper needs step ordering)
- Context should carry: `value` (current active step), `onValueChange` callback, `orientation`, and a list of registered step values for ordering/completion detection
- Support `data-state="active"` on the active StepperItem, `data-state="completed"` on completed steps (those before the active step)
- `StepperTrigger` should have `data-state` matching its item (active/completed/inactive)
- `StepperContent` only renders (or shows) when its step is active

**Orientation:**
- `horizontal`: steps laid out in a row, separators are horizontal lines between trigger areas
- `vertical`: steps laid out in a column, separators are vertical lines on the left side

**Styling conventions (using Tailwind):**
- Use `data-[orientation=horizontal]` / `data-[orientation=vertical]` on the root and propagate via `group/stepper` class and `group-data-[orientation=...]/stepper` selectors in child components
- Use `data-[state=active]` / `data-[state=completed]` / `data-[state=inactive]` for styling step states
- Step indicator/circle: a numbered circle (1, 2, 3...) showing in StepperTrigger — completed steps show a check icon (from lucide-react)
- The trigger area contains: indicator circle + title/description side by side

**Demo:**
- Show a 3-step horizontal wizard (e.g., "Account Setup" → "Personal Details" → "Review")
- Use `useState` to manage the active step
- Show Next/Back buttons inside each StepperContent panel using the `button` component
- The demo should be interactive and realistic

## Plan Requirements

Please provide:
1. **Component architecture** — exactly which functions/types to define, in what order
2. **Context design** — TypeScript interface for the context value, how step registration works
3. **Each sub-component** — its props, className structure, data attributes, Tailwind classes for each state
4. **Separator logic** — how/where StepperSeparator is rendered relative to StepperItem  
5. **Registry entries** — exact format for both `ui/_registry.ts` and `examples/_registry.ts`
6. **Demo design** — what the demo should show, what state it manages, what imports it needs
7. **Files to create** (2 new files) and **files to modify** (2 registry files — by appending entries)
8. **Insertion points** — where exactly in `ui/_registry.ts` (alphabetically, "stepper" goes after "spinner") and in `examples/_registry.ts` (append at the end before the closing `]`)
9. **Verification** — how to confirm the component works after implementation

10	const ToggleGroupContext = React.createContext<
11	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
12	    spacing?: number
13	  }
14	>({
15	  size: "default",
16	  variant: "default",
17	  spacing: 0,
18	})
19	
20	function ToggleGroup({
21	  className,
22	  variant,
23	  size,
24	  spacing = 0,
25	  children,
26	  ...props
27	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root> &
28	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
29	    spacing?: number
30	  }) {
31	  return (
32	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root
33	      data-slot="toggle-group"
34	      data-variant={variant}
35	      data-size={size}
36	      data-spacing={spacing}
37	      style={{ "--gap": spacing } as React.CSSProperties}
38	      className={cn(
39	        "group/toggle-group flex w-fit items-center gap-[--spacing(var(--gap))] rounded-md data-[spacing=default]:data-[variant=outline]:shadow-xs",
40	        className
41	      )}
42	      {...props}
43	    >
44	      <ToggleGroupContext.Provider value={{ variant, size, spacing }}>
45	        {children}
46	      </ToggleGroupContext.Provider>
47	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root>
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	function ToggleGroupItem({
52	  className,
53	  children,
54	  variant,
55	  size,
56	  ...props
57	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item> &
58	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants>) {
59	  const context = React.useContext(ToggleGroupContext)
60	
61	  return (
62	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item
63	      data-slot="toggle-group-item"
64	      data-variant={context.variant || variant}
65	      data-size={context.size || size}
66	      data-spacing={context.spacing}
67	      className={cn(
68	        toggleVariants({
69	          variant: context.variant || variant,
70	          size: context.size || size,
71	        }),
72	        "w-auto min-w-0 shrink-0 px-3 focus:z-10 focus-visible:z-10",
73	        "data-[spacing=0]:rounded-none data-[spacing=0]:shadow-none data-[spacing=0]:first:rounded-l-md data-[spacing=0]:last:rounded-r-md data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:border-l-0 data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:first:border-l",
74	        className
75	      )}
76	      {...props}
77	    >
78	      {children}
79	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item>
80	  )
81	}
82	
83	export { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem }

59	      {...props}
60	    />
61	  )
62	}
63	
64	export { Button, buttonVariants }
